title: Leetcode 3453. Separate Squares I
shortDescription: Given axis-aligned squares (xi, yi, li) whose overlaps count multiply, find the smallest horizontal line y such that the sum of areas of all squares above y equals the sum below. The core approach is to exploit that the area difference is monotonic in y and binary-search the y-coordinate, computing each square's above/below contribution by its vertical overlap.

Leetcode 3453. Separate Squares I

Given axis-aligned squares (xi, yi, li) whose overlaps count multiply, find the smallest horizontal line y such that the sum of areas of all squares above y equals the sum below. The core approach is to exploit that the area difference is monotonic in y and binary-search the y-coordinate, computing each square's above/below contribution by its vertical overlap.
